title: The Scarlet Grove
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:'''"The Scarlet Grove"'''}} {{Background|file=Red forest trees.jpg}} __NOTOC__ {{Environmentalism-Volcanically Active Theme}} {{CSS|content= @import url(https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Raleway:ital,wght@0,100..900;1,100..900&display=swap); }}
She opens her eyes and instantly light pours into them.
{{Blurimage|file=ScarletGrovebackground.webp|caption=...|align=center}}
When her eyes adjust, Frost sees off-white clouds form an overcast sky above. She feels grass below her, but it is oddly soft and not remotely unpleasant to lie on. Slowly sitting up, her eyes are bombarded with more shades of red than she ever knew existed. She’s encircled by vibrant trees with rose-colored leaves and burgundy trunks. She hears distant birdsong and the rustle of leaves in the wind. Frost takes a breath allowing a waft of pleasant floral aroma into her lungs. She knows she’s heard of this level, and its naturality somehow makes her uneasy. Quickly standing up she pulled her phone out of the bag and turned it on.

No signal, not that she expected to have it, unaware that she failed to regard the phone’s extremely low battery. First, she navigates to the camera app and takes a photo of her surroundings. She knew she kept records of some levels, files, and objects but she didn’t know if she had this one. Unable to remember the name exactly, she input multiple words into her search bar, till at last the one she wanted came up. She excitedly opened it.
{{Class Variable|safety=Mysterious Properties|security=Unknown Information|entity=Undocumented Entities|class=Undetermined|hexagon=?|color-h=3|color-l=30%|color-s=100%}} '''The Scarlet Grove''' is an anomalous level of the Backrooms. {{Blurimage|file=Red fog.jpg|caption=One of a few known images of The Scarlet Grove.}} ==Description== '''The Scarlet Grove''' is an old-growth forest entirely comprised of red terrain and plants. Small to medium-sized trees take up a large amount of the surface area and can seemingly be any type of tree from the Frontrooms. Deciduous trees are observed to have numerous leaves despite the vast amounts of fallen ones below them indicating possible regenerative abilities. Less notable flora such as shrubs are usually bunched up against the trees. Moss and lichen can often be found growing on trunks and rocks. The '''Scarlet Grove''' has been described as having a sort of natural beauty wanderers often state is due to the high amount of plant life.

The sky in '''The Scarlet Grove''' is devoid of celestial bodies
and exists in a permanent state of overcast. Despite this,
sunbeams frequently slice through the trees from no
apparent source. Most of '''The Scarlet Grove''' appears
{{Blurimage|file=Deadbattery2.webp|caption= |align=center}}
And then the phone dies.

She blinks, staring in shock before she throws the phone down and begins to curse herself and her luck. Frost quickly collects herself and reaches down to pick up her phone, wiping dirt off the screen and exchanging it within her bag for a handgun. A Temperate breeze flows through her hair, and examines the pistol curiously, turning it over in her hand before clutching it against her chest. You watch as Frost takes her first steps forward into your domain.

After a short walk, she passes through the threshold of the treeline. Although there was no path, the trees were small and far enough apart that Frost could catch many glimpses of the sky through the leaves. Her boots scrape against small crimson shrubs and tall violet grass. She runs a hand across the soft bark of what appears to be a magnolia tree in a strange shade of red and the feeling tickles.

She crouches down and inspects the rough texture of lichen on the trunk which is a pretty shade of white instead of the uniform red of everything else. Frost notes a weak point where the bark had peeled and gently pulls it back to reveal nothing but more mahogany-colored bark underneath. She sighs in relief and stands back up. {{Blurimage|file=Sun shining through a red forest.jpg|caption=Do you think it's beautiful, Dear?|align=center|hovertext=Welcome home}}
Frost looked around, unable to pick a direction to move in. She was very flighty and when a nearby bush rustled slightly she clutched her pistol, only drawing a finger away from the trigger when it turned out to be the wind. She started walking in a seemingly random direction after that, although still on edge with the gun pointed in front of her. The speed and twitchiness with which she walked over stones and gnarled roots made you sure she would trip, but Frost was more nimble than expected.

Her adrenaline seems to fade over time and her movements slow until she reluctantly holstered the pistol and walked at a normal pace. Frost surprisingly seems to take in the beauty of the forest around her. The branches and their leaves above created a thin natural ceiling blocking the sky, which thin but sturdy tree trunks grew towards. Grass on the forest floor sways in the breeze as fallen leaves crunch under her feet. As she listened for more birdsong, faint bubbling noises draw her attention towards a small brook. Her unease was fading, which would make your work much easier. {{Blurimage|file=Gloriusredgrove.png|caption=I made it just for you.|align=center|hovertext=Please don't cry}}
She followed the small brook downstream, as it curled around hills and tumbled over rocks, its precious water giving life to tiny plants beside it. She ran a finger through the cool lapping flow on multiple occasions, letting it rejuvenate her spirit. As she kept walking alongside it, she noticed that the sound of lapping got louder up ahead. A vigor filled her step and she eagerly proceeded. She broke through the treeline and before her the forest opened up to the sky and her gaze connects to ruby trees lying across a shining expanse of water.

The water is crystal clear and shows her everything lying below it. She sees flat, round stones and what appears to be auburn algae, their tendril-like leaves bobbing in the swell of water. Frost just pauses, taking it all in. Her body relaxes as she sits down, and she gives a genuine smile to the world. Now, since she's relaxed and has practically forgotten the pistol by her side, it was time for you to make your move. {{Blurimage|file=RedlakeShirogane.jpg|caption=I'll always be there for you.|align=center|hovertext=Even though I can't change the way things are}}
You call out to her by name and she jumps. She remembers the pistol and wildly swings it, trying to identify where you are.
Her eyes are fearful as she responds. “Who’s there? Show yourself.” Her voice is strangely calm, although tinged with repressed terror.
You tell her that you are all around her; that you are the grove. You make your voice soothing so that she won't be afraid anymore.
She seems confused by this response. "So, this level is alive and you're the level? Am I trapped here forever or something? Are you going to subject me to some form of cruel torture?" Frost lowers her gun, but her guard is still up.
You assure her there's going to be no torturing. You lament to her that your time together will be fleeting.
She glances around her, still visibly uncomfortable but putting on a brave face. "Is that some sort of threat?"
You tell her that it's not any more of a threat than someone trying to take on an entire forest with a handgun.
She laughs at your reply, although she's still a bit uneasy. "I'll take stupid options if they're the only options that keep me alive."
You respond with a sad laugh. You ask her to pull out her phone.
Frost is confused by this but obliges, nonetheless. With a single thought, you recharge it to full battery and show her an excerpt of your article.
==Entrances and Exits== '''The Scarlet Grove''' is accessible from any Backrooms level. The only known method of entry is believed to be some form of astral projection, as Wanderers who enter are still present at the level of entry. Despite this, wanderers will retain all physical items they have on their person which if altered or damaged during their time in '''The Scarlet Grove''' will reflect that acquired state on egress. Entry into '''The Scarlet Grove''' operates like a near-death experience and is only accessible for mere minutes or seconds of real-time. Although those inside usually experience much longer durations up to several hours. Only ~8% of NDEs recorded within the Backrooms will result in transportation to '''The Scarlet Grove'''. Patterns exist amongst successful entrants, with 96% reporting feelings of being unloved, 82% reporting a lack of purpose, and 70% suffering from clinical depression. It is unknown if permanently fatal incidents can result in travel to '''The Scarlet Grove'''. After the termination of a visit, one will wake up at the level and place of entry.
"Oh..." Frost stares at her phone, before turning it off and slipping it back in her bag. "Am I going back?"
You wish you could tell her yes, but you know she needs to know the truth. Her time alive is up, and her time here is running out. But that at least for now you're here for her.
She takes this better than you expected, even if she looks wistful. "What happens when times up?"
You explain that you'd guide them to a place where they can pass on.
She sighs and at last holsters her gun. "I'd rather go out on my own terms than wait for an invisible clock to run out."
You tell her you understand. Effortlessly, you transport her to a stone path path that leads to the threshold. The threshold itself is a wooden gate in a Japanese architectural style. It is small and a pale brown color.
"That's it? I expected something more grandiose." Frost comments.
You make the air itself wrap around her in a parting hug before telling her one last thing.
{{Blurimage|file=Red forest path to shrine.jpg|caption=I love you.|hovertext=No matter what, Lydia.|align=center}}
